zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe於1234年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿申維爾索普時，他的父親John de Thorpe66歲，母親Margery de Creke45歲。他於大約1265年與Maud De Eyre結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1304年享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).
Altered form of German   Trapp  .

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .
Scandinavian: variant of   Torp  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Tharp
meanings: English: variant of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: FitzJohn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name John , ‘son of John’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

variants.name: Fidgeon
meanings: 1  English: (i) variant of   Vivian  .  Compare  Videan  . (ii) variant of   Fitzjohn  .
2 Irish: reduced variant of Mag Guagáin, see McGuigan .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe was born in 1234, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Thorpe, was 66 and his mother, Margery de Creke, was 45. He married Maud De Eyre about 1265. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1304, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe est né en 1234, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Thorpe, avait 66 ans et sa mère, Margery de Creke, avait 45 ans. Il a épousé Maud De Eyre environ 1265. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1304, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe 1234 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Thorpe 66 und seine Mutter Margery de Creke 45. Er hat um 1265 Maud De Eyre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1304 im Alter von 70 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe è nato il nel 1234 nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Thorpe, aveva 66 anni e il suo madre, Margery de Creke, aveva 45 anni. Lui sposato Maud De Eyre circa 1265. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1304, all'età di 70 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe(이)가 1234년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 애스h웰소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Thorpe(은)는 66세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery de Creke(은)는 45세였다. 그는 대략 1265년 Maud De Eyre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1304년 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe nasceu em 1234, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Thorpe, tinha 66 anos e sua mãe, Margery de Creke, tinha 45 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud De Eyre aproximadamente 1265. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1304, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe родился в 1234 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Thorpe было 66 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery de Creke, 45. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud De Eyre около 1265 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1304 г. в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert FitzJohn De Thorpe nació en 1234, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Thorpe, tenía 66 años y su mamá, Margery de Creke, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Maud De Eyre aproximadamente 1265. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1304, a la edad de 70 años.
